parsed_review.summary: CP00-520 is a descriptor-only AI Governance (RP17) permission-slice pack covering RP17.P02.M05.S01-S10 (risk class A, 10 units, micro M05 "Permission And Audit Binding"). Independent read-only inspection confirms full internal consistency across the plan binding (closeout-pack-plan.json head pack), manifest, registry/service/validators implementation, contract projection (p02_permission_slice_descriptor, 10 rows), unit tests, and the rp17 validator script — all asserting descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false per row, with historical_pack_bindings terminating at CP00-520 and no future-pack (CP00-521) descriptor leakage. The pack-level production_ready claim is honest and well-supported: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review; the production_ready_flag asserts only descriptor verification, not runtime/enterprise trust. All no-write / no-real-data / synthetic-only / no-leak attestations are consistently set, and the two security_audit rows (Permission precheck, Audit hint precheck) remain pure descriptors with no permission-decision or audit-body leakage. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob, read_only=true), claude_is_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false throughout; human final approval remains required. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied (auth preflight passed with valid JSON, no forbidden tools, schema present). changed-file-scope correctly separates the 8 active pack implementation refs from unrelated dirty control-room UI files (progress-control-room-data.mjs / index.html), which contain no pack references. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are legitimately absent because this read-only review is the step that gates their production. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
